reasoning: Finland is incredibly peaceful with political violence fatalities historically near 0. The ACLED baseline on Oct 30, 2025, indicated 0 fatalities over the previous year. For the question to resolve positively, there must be at least 11 fatalities (as 10 x 1 = 10, thus >10 means 11+) from political violence within a 30-day window leading up to each resolution date. Extensive searches of news and events leading up to the November 9, 2025 cutoff reveal no evidence of recent fatal terrorist attacks, riots, or border clashes, though there is general monitoring of the Russian border and recent military drills. Therefore, the likelihood of 11+ fatalities occurring in those 30-day periods is exceptionally small. I place probabilities at the lowest allowed bounds, increasing slightly for more distant dates (resolutions in late 2025 and early 2026) to reflect growing temporal uncertainty.
